cite: SF Municipal Reports FY1888-89 (Registrar master table, p.856) — Nov 2 1886 general: registration 48,792, total vote 45,716

SF Municipal Reports vol47 Board of Election Commissioners' Report p.277 cumulative table (mirror/muni_reports/vol47_n320.jpg, operator hand-read 2026-07-07): Nov 4 1890 general, 310 precincts, registration 59,770, total vote 55,565 — upgrades the prior stitched floor (Great Register 59,712 + Governor-sum 55,317, SOV/Blue Book 1891 p.88, kept here for the record) with the registrar's own official series, which continues past the FY1888-89 table's 1888 endpoint

SF Municipal Reports Dept. of Elections cumulative registration-and-votes table; confirmed identical in four printings read from page images (vol49 FY1898-99 p.55, vol53 FY1902-03 p.290, vol55 FY1904-05 p.264, vol57 FY1907-08 p.871, archive.org sanfranciscomuni49/53/55/57sanfrich; evidence in mirror/muni_reports/) - Nov 8 1892 presidential general
Municipal Reports cumulative table; confirmed identical in four printings (vols 49/53/55/57, page images in mirror/muni_reports/) - Nov 6 1894 general

Municipal Reports cumulative table; ballots 64,820 per the three earliest printings including the election's own fiscal-year volume (vol47 FY1896-97 p.277 image-read, vol49, vol53); vols 55/57 print 61,820, a propagated compositor error ruled out arithmetically by the SOV presidential-elector sum 61,889 (statementofvoteo1886cali leaves n110-n113); arbitration in .superpowers evidence and mirror/muni_reports/vol47_n320.jpg - Nov 3 1896 presidential general

Municipal Reports cumulative table; confirmed in vols 53/55/57 (vol49 alone prints registration 62,410, a compositor slip duplicating the adjacent Aug 1899 primary row's figure) - Nov 8 1898 general; the municipal offices (Mayor Phelan re-elected) rode the state ballot, there was no separate Nov 1 municipal election; registration dip vs 1897 reflects the fresh biennial register

1916-11-07 general-election registration (SF DOE historical turnout table); biennial-roll era (Stats. 1899 / Pol. Code sec. 1094: complete new registration each even year; specials use the last-general roll plus supplement); roll had grown since the general close, so turnout slightly overstates

1916-11-07 general-election registration (SF DOE historical turnout table); biennial-roll era (Stats. 1899 / Pol. Code sec. 1094: complete new registration each even year; specials use the last-general roll plus supplement); roll had grown since the general close, so turnout slightly overstates

1920-11-02 general-election registration (SF DOE historical turnout table); biennial-roll era (Stats. 1899 / Pol. Code sec. 1094: complete new registration each even year; specials use the last-general roll plus supplement); roll of the 1920-21 biennium at its Nov 1920 close; turnout approximate

1922-11-07 general-election registration (SF DOE historical turnout table); biennial-roll era (Stats. 1899 / Pol. Code sec. 1094: complete new registration each even year; specials use the last-general roll plus supplement); two weeks after the general on the same roll; near-exact
